title: Chronic disease annual review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.
assessment.rationale: The exact multimorbidity section supports clinician review of conditions, treatment burden, function, frailty, medicines, and impact without assuming which chronic diseases are present.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation
source.population: Adults with two or more long-term conditions, including frailty, falls, sensory impairment, chronic pain, mental health conditions, and treatment burden.
source.setting: Primary and community care with person-centred multimorbidity review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting day-to-day function, frailty and falls context, treatment burden, multiple medicines, personal goals, values, carer involvement, and clinician-led treatment review. It does not auto-score frailty or recommend starting, stopping, or changing treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.4 — multimorbidity, daily activities, falls, medicines, and frailty context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

assessment.text: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.
assessment.rationale: The exact priorities section supports patient goals, preferences, quality of life, coordination, and agreed priorities without generating disease-specific management.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation
source.population: Adults with two or more long-term conditions, including frailty, falls, sensory impairment, chronic pain, mental health conditions, and treatment burden.
source.setting: Primary and community care with person-centred multimorbidity review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting day-to-day function, frailty and falls context, treatment burden, multiple medicines, personal goals, values, carer involvement, and clinician-led treatment review. It does not auto-score frailty or recommend starting, stopping, or changing treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — treatment burden, day-to-day life, carers, goals, values, and priorities

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-led review of benefits, harms, treatment burden, personal priorities, and a documented discussion about reducing or stopping treatment without generating a medication change.
assessment.rationale: The exact treatment-review section supports recording reviewed results, medicine burden, clinician-entered plan, communication, and follow-up without changing treatment automatically.
